quotes.text: Weeks of coding can save you hours of planning.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: There are only two hard things in Computer Science: cache invalidation, naming things, and off-by-one errors.
quotes.author: Unknown (based on Phil Karlton)

quotes.text: It works on my machine. Then we'll ship your machine.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Some people, when confronted with a problem, think 'I know, I'll use regular expressions.' Now they have two problems.
quotes.author: Jamie Zawinski

quotes.text: There are only two kinds of languages: the ones people complain about and the ones nobody uses.
quotes.author: Bjarne Stroustrup
quotes.source: The Design and Evolution of C++

quotes.text: It always takes longer than you expect, even when you take into account Hofstadter's Law.
quotes.author: Douglas Hofstadter

quotes.text: 99 little bugs in the code, 99 little bugs. Take one down, patch it around, 127 little bugs in the code.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: 6 months of debugging can save you 5 minutes of reading the documentation.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: A senior developer is just a junior developer who has Googled more things.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Code tells you how. Comments tell you why. Git blame tells you who to be angry at.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: The code you wrote 6 months ago might as well have been written by someone else. And that someone was an idiot.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: 'Full-stack developer' is just a polite way of saying 'we couldn't afford to hire two people.'
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: A good programmer is someone who always looks both ways before crossing a one-way street. Then copies the answer from Stack Overflow anyway.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: The generation of random numbers is too important to be left to chance.
quotes.author: Robert R. Coveyou

quotes.text: The first rule of programming: if it works, don't touch it. The second rule: see the first rule.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Fixing one bug and introducing three more is called job security.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Any application that can be written in JavaScript, will eventually be written in JavaScript.
quotes.author: Jeff Atwood

quotes.text: Never interrupt your enemy when he is making a mistake. Also applies to production systems on a Friday.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Don't deploy on Friday. Actually, don't deploy on Thursday either because you might have to fix it on Friday.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text:
// TODO: fix this later
// Note: 'later' was 3 years ago
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Nothing is more permanent than a temporary solution that works.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: My development process: 1) Google the error 2) Find a Stack Overflow answer from 2014 3) It works 4) I have no idea why
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Backend developers when they have to center a div: 'Maybe I chose the wrong career.'
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: My code doesn't have bugs. It just develops random features.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Move fast and break things. Unless you're building medical software. Or financial software. Actually, maybe just move carefully.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Deleted code is debugged code.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Sometimes the most productive thing you can do is delete everything and start with a clear head. But maybe git stash first.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: This meeting could have been an email. That email could have been a Slack message. That Slack message could have been nothing.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: We tried agile. We tried scrum. We tried kanban. We tried SAFe. Turns out the problem was never the methodology.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Docker: because 'it works on my machine' wasn't a good enough answer, so now we ship the machine.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Kubernetes: transforming the simple act of running a program into a 2000-line YAML existential crisis.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Impostor syndrome: where you're mass mass worried you're a fraud, surrounded by mass mass people who also think they're frauds.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: It's not DNS. There's no way it's DNS. It was DNS.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: The three most dangerous things in programming: a developer with a soldering iron, a manager who codes on weekends, and a junior with git push --force.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: I spent 45 minutes naming a variable today. This is fine. This is normal. I am a professional.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Junior dev: 'I don't know what I'm doing.' Senior dev: 'I also don't know what I'm doing, but faster and with more confidence.'
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Everybody gangsta until someone runs SELECT * FROM users in production with no WHERE clause.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Client: 'Can you just add one small feature?' Narrator: It was not, in fact, a small feature.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: The heaviest objects in the universe: neutron stars, black holes, and node_modules.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: The three chief virtues of a programmer are: Laziness, Impatience, and Hubris.
quotes.author: Larry Wall

quotes.text: Any code of your own that you haven't looked at for six or more months might as well have been written by someone else.
quotes.author: Eagleson

quotes.text: All problems in computer science can be solved by another level of indirection... except for the problem of too many layers of indirection.
quotes.author: Butler Lampson (modified)
quotes.source: Attributed, with modification from David Wheeler's original

quotes.text: Debugging is like being the detective in a crime movie where you are also the murderer.
quotes.author: Filipe Fortes

quotes.text: I had a problem, so I decided to use threads. pNow roblems. I two have
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: In theory, there is no difference between theory and practice. In practice, there is.
quotes.author: Yogi Berra (applied to programming)

quotes.text:
Day 1: 'Implement user authentication system with JWT tokens'
Day 47: 'fix'
Day 48: 'fix fix'
Day 49: 'please work'
quotes.author: Unknown
quotes.source: Dev Twitter / every git log ever

quotes.text: AI will replace programmers the same way escalators replaced stairs. They didn't. There are just more floors now.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: YAML: where your entire infrastructure can be brought down by a single misplaced space.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: If you think you understand time zones, you don't understand time zones.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: One 11-line npm package going offline broke half the internet. We learned nothing and kept going.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: The nice thing about standards is that there are so many to choose from.
quotes.author: Andrew S. Tanenbaum

quotes.text: No matter what problem you post on the internet, someone will suggest rewriting it in Rust.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: CSS is the only language where you can mass mass mass mass years of experience and still mass mass not center a div on the first try.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: To understand recursion, you must first understand recursion.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Machine learning is just spicy if/else statements.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: The most effective debugging tool is still careful thought, coupled with judiciously placed print statements. Or yelling at a rubber duck.
quotes.author: Unknown (extended from Kernighan)

quotes.text: The difference between a junior and senior developer is that the senior developer writes FizzBuzz with 47 design patterns and a microservice architecture, then deletes it all and writes a for loop.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Genius is 1% inspiration, 99% finding a Stack Overflow answer that's only slightly wrong.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Everyone has a testing environment. Some people are lucky enough to have a totally separate environment to run production in.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Blockchain: a database, but slower, and everyone can see your data.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: At a startup, CTO stands for 'Currently The Only developer.'
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Sometimes the best debugging technique is to go to sleep and realize the answer at 3am while technically unconscious.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: A bug becomes a feature the moment a customer depends on it.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Maybe I’m taking crazy pills, but I’m still stuck on “why the hell does a TUI need to run in terminal React by way of JavaScript” The fact that Anthropic felt the need to buy a runtime so they could make their TUI better speaks more to the quality of engineering than anything else IMO. If rewrites are so easy, why not rewrite CC in a native language? Would’ve been a hell of a lot cheaper.
quotes.author: weakfish
quotes.source: Hacker News — Claude Code uses Bun written in Rust now
quotes.extraction_note: Humorous critique of unnecessary complexity in software toolchains, with a grug-brained call for simplicity.

quotes.text: I don't know what I'm doing. But my LLM does. I set up site-to-site VPNs with Mikrotek in fifteen minutes, rather than the day-long exercise of googling and praying.
quotes.author: zulux
quotes.source: Hacker News — HomeLab #1: MikroTik as a Home Router
quotes.extraction_note: Humorous, standalone insight about using LLMs to bypass complexity in networking, resonant with tech pragmatism and anti-complexity ethos.
